skill_directive.template: Call the `skill` tool with `{ name: "{bundle}-{skill}" }` before continuing.
skill_directive.note: Transform 1: a full-line `Skill: {bundle}:{skill}` directive rewrites to an OpenCode `skill`-tool call. `{bundle}` / `{skill}` are substituted from the source directive; the emitter's `{bundle}-{skill}` namespacing makes the load target resolve. The full-line matcher (SKILL_DIRECTIVE_RE) is owned by the shared engine, so only this template is target data.

slash_command.note: Transform 2: `/skill-name` cross-references rewrite to `/{bundle}-{skill}` (the dual-emit command id). `{name}` is the namespaced form resolved from the marketplace user-invocable scan. The bare-name matcher is owned by the shared engine, so only this template is target data.

AskUserQuestion.note: Backtick-wrapped `AskUserQuestion` tool references rewrite to the OpenCode `question` tool. Bare prose mentions of the escalation mechanism are left as the concept name.

Task:.note: Leaf-aware: the dispatcher's leaf-constraint prose ('no Task: dispatch', 'every plan-marshall Task: invocation') is descriptive and MUST NOT be blanket-rewritten to `task`. Deliberate non-rewrite per doc 06 item 3.

Skill: <entry>.note: The `<entry>` runtime placeholder is reworded target-neutrally in the source (the step-3 skill-load loop in agents/execution-context.md) rather than rewritten at emit time, so no emit-time rewrite is needed.

Monitor.note: The background-completion watch has no OpenCode analog, so there is no tool name to rewrite to. The occurrences are body prose naming the concept, which `rewrite_inline_code` deliberately does not touch — they are reworded to target-neutral vocabulary in the source instead. Paired with the `tool_permissions` target-absent (`null`) entry.
